title: eucalyptus tereticornis subsp. basaltica
intro: Eucalyptus tereticornis subsp. basaltica, also known as the New South Wales peppermint, is a subspecies of eucalyptus native to the eastern coast of Australia. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, derived from its essential oils. The health benefits of Eucalyptus tereticornis subsp. basaltica include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. In herbal preparations, the leaves can be used in teas, infusions, and topical applications to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment.
